description: Calculate parcel-level lot coverage and aggregate it to identify San Francisco's least permeable 'Grey Zones' using the Jupyter notebook at /home/ga/urbansim_projects/notebooks/lot_coverage_analysis.ipynb. Using the 'buildings' and 'parcels' tables from /home/ga/urbansim_projects/data/sanfran_public.h5, estimate building footprints (filling missing building_sqft with (residential_units * 1000) + non_residential_sqft and missing stories with 1) to determine parcel lot coverage ratios capped at 1.0. Aggregate these metrics by zone_id to calculate average lot coverage, total parcel area, total footprint area, and valid parcel counts, including only zones with at least 30 valid parcels. Export the results to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/zone_lot_coverage.csv, a summary JSON (with keys: citywide_avg_coverage, num_zones_analyzed, greyest_zone_id, greenest_zone_id) to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/coverage_summary.json, and a histogram of average lot coverage to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/coverage_histogram.png. The notebook must be fully executed and saved.
